questions.question: What distinguishes Augmented Reality (AR) from Virtual Reality (VR)?
options: AR replaces the real world entirely with a virtual one
AR enhances the real world with digital overlays while you still see your surroundings
AR only works with head‑mounted displays
AR can only be used with printed markers
questions.explanation: AR keeps the real environment visible and adds digital content on top of it. VR, in contrast, typically blocks out the real world and shows only a virtual environment.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common everyday example of AR?
options: Watching a pre‑recorded 2D video on your phone
Using a social media filter that adds virtual glasses and masks to your face in real time
Listening to a podcast while walking
Reading a static infographic on a website
questions.explanation: Face filters track your face in the camera image and overlay virtual elements on top of the real video feed, which is a typical AR use case.

questions.question: At a high level, what does an AR system need to do before it can place virtual objects correctly in the real world?
options: Only capture a single still image from the camera
Estimate the device's motion and understand enough about the environment to anchor content
Download a 3D model from the internet
Increase the screen brightness to maximum
questions.explanation: AR systems typically estimate the device's position and orientation (motion tracking) and detect surfaces, feature points, or markers so virtual content can be anchored consistently.

questions.question: Which statement best describes marker‑based AR?
options: It only uses GPS to place objects in the world
It relies on recognizing specific images or patterns to anchor content
It requires depth sensors for every scenario
It never uses the device camera
questions.explanation: Marker‑based AR recognizes predefined images or patterns (such as printed cards or logos) and uses them as anchors for virtual content.

questions.question: Which of these platforms provides AR capabilities on Android devices?
options: Google ARCore
Apple ARKit
Vuforia only
Unity AR Foundation only
questions.explanation: Google ARCore is Google's AR platform for supported Android devices. Unity AR Foundation can also target Android, but it typically does so by using ARCore or other underlying providers.

questions.question: What is the main goal of Unity's AR Foundation framework?
options: To replace all native AR SDKs entirely
To provide a unified API in Unity that can work with multiple AR providers such as ARCore and ARKit
To add ray tracing to every AR application
To create 2D user interfaces only
questions.explanation: AR Foundation is designed to give Unity developers a single C# API that can target multiple underlying AR SDKs (like ARCore and ARKit), reducing duplicate work across platforms.
